criteria.name: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every interactive control (STORE, PRESS KIT, hamburger, menu items, socials, legal links, shortlist controls, race rows, status filter, Undo, Redo, email field, Subscribe, palette search/results) is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter/Space).
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Several icon-only buttons and links have no accessible name, so keyboard operation of every required control is not fully established. Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_modal_focus_trap_and_return
criteria.description: The menu overlay behaves as a modal dialog: it traps focus while open, closes on Escape, and returns focus to the hamburger button on close.
criteria.reasoning: Menu focus cycled within the dialog, Escape closed it, and focus returned to the hamburger button. Evidence: menu_modal_focus_trap_and_return-shared.png

criteria.name: media_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Editorial images, helmet cards, partner marks, and the social video poster carry descriptive alt text or accessible labels where they convey content.
criteria.reasoning: Editorial images, primary helmet artwork, partner marks, and the social poster exposed descriptive alt text; decorative secondary images were empty-alt. Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: newsletter_feedback_uses_live_region
criteria.description: Newsletter inline error and confirmation messages are announced through an aria-live region as well as shown visually.
criteria.reasoning: Invalid email feedback appeared visually and in an aria-live status region. Evidence: newsletter_feedback_uses_live_region-shared.png

criteria.name: newsletter_field_has_explicit_label
criteria.description: The newsletter email field uses an explicit label association (or equivalent accessible name).
criteria.reasoning: The newsletter input has an associated label and accessible name “Join the Nova Racing dispatch.” Evidence: newsletter_feedback_uses_live_region-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_logical_order
criteria.description: Section headings follow a logical heading order through the homepage with no skipped levels that break the outline.
criteria.reasoning: The page uses one H1 followed by H2 sections, with only a valid H2-to-H3 progression in the footer structure. Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: landmarks_nav_main_footer
criteria.description: The document exposes HTML-first landmarks: header/nav chrome, a main content landmark wrapping the homepage sections, and a footer landmark.
criteria.reasoning: Header, navigation, main, and footer landmarks were directly observed. Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: contrast_cream_and_dark_sections
criteria.description: Text over the cream hero and dark-green sections, and all control labels, meet WCAG AA contrast; lime STORE button text on lime stays high-contrast (dark text on rgb(210, 255, 0)).
criteria.reasoning: Observed cream/dark section text and lime controls use high-contrast dark or light foreground colors, including dark text on rgb(210,255,0). Evidence: contrast_cream_and_dark_sections-shared.png

criteria.name: semantic_interactive_chrome
criteria.description: Interactive chrome uses real controls (button, label, native links) with semantic roles; custom overlays expose matching ARIA roles only where native elements cannot carry the behavior.
criteria.reasoning: Interactive chrome uses native buttons, links, inputs, tabs, and explicitly labeled dialog roles. Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: reduced_motion_settles_immediately
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, scroll-linked animation settles immediately, the LOAD VALE preloader does not block content, in-page jumps use instant scroll, and continuous marquees do not run — while the page remains fully scrollable and operable.
criteria.reasoning: In the reduced-motion browser, prefers-reduced-motion matched, scroll behavior was auto, animation durations were reduced to near-zero, the preloader did not block content, and real keyboard paging reached the document bottom. Evidence: reduced_motion_settles_immediately-shared.png

criteria.name: split_text_aria_label_on_container
criteria.description: Split-text headlines (impact statement and footer brand statement) keep the original phrase as an aria-label on the heading container while individual character/word spans are aria-hidden from the accessibility tree.
criteria.reasoning: The split NO LIMITS and footer statement headings retained full aria-label phrases, with their child spans aria-hidden. Evidence: contrast_cream_and_dark_sections-shared.png

criteria.name: hero_webgl_labelled_or_decorative
criteria.description: The hero canvas or WebGL layer is treated as decorative or labelled: role=img with a descriptive aria-label, or aria-hidden when a static silhouette sibling already conveys the artwork.
criteria.reasoning: The hero canvas exposed role=img with the descriptive label “Abstract Avery Vale racer composition.” Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: press_kit_and_palette_modal_a11y
criteria.description: The press-kit drawer and command palette each behave as modal dialogs: Escape closes them, focus is trapped while open, and focus returns to the control that opened them.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The command palette opened and closed, but the press-kit drawer did not close on Escape; therefore both modal workflows were not fully satisfied. Evidence: press_kit_and_palette_modal_a11y-shared.png

criteria.name: shortlist_controls_accessible_names
criteria.description: Icon-only shortlist controls on helmet and editorial cards expose descriptive accessible names.
criteria.reasoning: Helmet and editorial shortlist buttons exposed descriptive names such as “Add to shortlist: ON TRACK” and “Add to shortlist: AURA GEOMETRY.” Evidence: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable-shared.png

criteria.name: selection_counts_live_region
criteria.description: Selected races N and shortlist count changes are announced via an aria-live region as well as shown visually.
criteria.reasoning: After selecting a race and shortlisting an editorial card, visible counts changed to Selected races 1 and Shortlist 1, and both announcements appeared in aria-live regions. Evidence: selection_counts_live_region-shared.png

criteria.name: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_resets
criteria.description: Starting on the homepage: open the menu, select a race, shortlist a helmet, type a valid email into the newsletter field, and hover the social video card; then reload. All facets coherently reset (menu/press kit/palette closed, selections and shortlist empty, newsletter empty with no confirmation, video not mid-hover-play) — never a mixed partial survival. In-memory genre: nothing persists.
criteria.reasoning: Reload reset menu, selections, shortlist, press kit, newsletter, and video playback state. Evidence: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_resets-after.png

criteria.name: subscribe_enablement_tracks_email_validity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: type an invalid email and confirm Subscribe stays disabled with an inline error; correct to a valid email and confirm the error clears and Subscribe enables — the control state changes meaningfully with the input.
criteria.reasoning: Invalid input showed an inline error and disabled Subscribe; correcting it cleared the error and enabled Subscribe. Evidence: subscribe_enablement_tracks_email_validity-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_home_stroke_echo
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: open the menu and confirm HOME shows the lime telemetry stroke while ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR do not; close and reopen without reload — HOME remains the current stroked item.
criteria.reasoning: HOME alone displayed the lime stroke; other menu items did not, and the state remained after close/reopen. Evidence: menu_home_stroke_echo-after.png

criteria.name: newsletter_confirmation_count_delta
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: with a valid email ready, note that zero confirmation messages are visible, submit Subscribe once, and confirm exactly one confirmation message appears with no off-by-one duplicate.
criteria.reasoning: One valid submission produced exactly one success confirmation and cleared the field. Evidence: newsletter_confirmation_count_delta-shared.png

criteria.name: invalid_vs_valid_email_outcomes_differ
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: attempt the newsletter flow with an invalid email (Subscribe disabled / error, no confirmation) then with a valid email (Subscribe enabled, submit shows confirmation and clears the field); the two outcomes differ as the inputs dictate.
criteria.reasoning: Invalid and valid emails produced the expected differing disabled/error versus enabled/success outcomes. Evidence: newsletter_confirmation_count_delta-shared.png

criteria.name: interleaved_menu_and_newsletter
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: open the menu, close it via X, type a valid email, open the menu again, close via Escape, then submit the newsletter; the confirmation appears and the menu remains closed — neither flow corrupted the other.
criteria.reasoning: Menu close/reopen and Escape interaction did not corrupt newsletter submission; confirmation appeared with menu closed. Evidence: newsletter_confirmation_count_delta-shared.png

criteria.name: video_hover_leave_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: hover the social video card (playback starts, placeholder fades), leave (paused, placeholder fully opaque), then hover again; playback and placeholder opacity track both transitions with no stuck intermediate state.
criteria.reasoning: Hover started playback and reduced poster opacity; leaving paused playback with opacity 1; re-hover restarted playback and faded the poster again. Evidence: video_hover_leave_round_trip-shared.png

criteria.name: press_kit_derived_sensitivity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: note the empty press-kit JSON preview, select Alpine GP and Bayfront Circuit and shortlist one helmet, reopen PRESS KIT; the JSON/Markdown now name those two circuits and the shortlisted label, and ICS has exactly two VEVENT entries — not an identical redraw.
criteria.reasoning: Press kit JSON named Alpine GP and Bayfront Circuit plus AURA GEOMETRY, and ICS contained exactly two VEVENT entries. Evidence: press_kit_derived_sensitivity-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_round_trip_race_select
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: select Solstice GP (Selected races increments), Undo (selection clears and count decrements), Redo (selection and count restore).
criteria.reasoning: Selecting Solstice raised the count, Undo reduced it, and Redo restored it. Evidence: undo_round_trip_race_select-shared.png

criteria.name: newsletter_echoes_into_press_kit
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: submit newsletter with fan@averyvale.example, open PRESS KIT JSON; the preview includes that subscriber email; a different valid email on a fresh flow yields a different email in the kit.
criteria.reasoning: The submitted email appeared in the press kit, and a fresh flow with a different valid email produced the different email. Evidence: newsletter_echoes_into_press_kit-shared.png

criteria.name: press_kit_import_round_trip_probe
criteria.description: Export then import round-trip probe: select Alpine GP and Bayfront Circuit and shortlist one helmet; copy JSON; Undo both selections and the shortlist; Import the JSON; Selected races is 2, shortlist count is 1, ICS has two VEVENT entries, and JSON races arrays name both circuits.
criteria.reasoning: After undoing the setup, importing the copied JSON restored 2 selected races and 1 shortlist item; ICS showed two VEVENT entries. Evidence: press_kit_import_round_trip_probe-after.png

criteria.name: status_enum_echoes_into_ics
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: with Ridgeway GP selected, Edit status to Upcoming; ICS STATUS for that VEVENT becomes CONFIRMED; set Completed and it becomes CANCELLED — not a stale STATUS.
criteria.reasoning: Ridgeway GP ICS STATUS changed to CONFIRMED for Upcoming and CANCELLED for Completed. Evidence: status_enum_echoes_into_ics-shared.png

criteria.name: single_page_homepage_title
criteria.description: On load the app serves a single homepage at route / (no other route is built), and the browser tab title reads exactly 2025 Apex Grand Prix Driver — Avery Vale
criteria.reasoning: Observed route / and exact browser title. Evidence: single_page_homepage_title-shared.png

criteria.name: preloader_shows_then_clears
criteria.description: On a fresh load a full-bleed lime preloader shows the text LOAD VALE and then clears within about one to two seconds to reveal the homepage rather than staying on screen blocking content
criteria.reasoning: Fresh reload showed full-viewport LOAD VALE at opacity 1, then it cleared while NEXT RACE became visible. Evidence: reload_baseline_probe-shared.png

criteria.name: fixed_nav_contents
criteria.description: A fixed nav stays pinned to the top over all sections and shows, left to right, a stacked AVERY VALE text wordmark (AVERY above VALE), a centered original AV monogram, a lime STORE button, a PRESS KIT control, and a square hamburger button
criteria.reasoning: Pinned nav visibly contains stacked wordmark, AV monogram, STORE, PRESS KIT, and hamburger. Evidence: hero_composition_with_silhouette-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_overlay_contents
criteria.description: After clicking the hamburger, a full-screen dark-green menu overlay covers the viewport listing HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, the social labels CLIPS, PHOTOS, VIDEO, LIVE, and the business contact hello@averyvale.example
criteria.reasoning: Open overlay visibly showed HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, CALENDAR, socials, contact, dark-green background, and photos. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: home_item_current_stroke
criteria.description: When the menu is open, the HOME item is marked as the current item with a lime telemetry stroke drawn through its label while ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, and CALENDAR are not
criteria.reasoning: HOME visibly had a lime stroke while the other primary items did not. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_closes_on_x_or_escape
criteria.description: After clicking the X control (or pressing Escape) in the open menu, the overlay closes, is no longer visible, and the homepage is interactive again
criteria.reasoning: X closed the menu and restored homepage interaction; Escape while closed was a no-op. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: next_race_widget_contents
criteria.description: The hero fills at least the first full viewport height and a NEXT RACE widget sits at its lower left showing the eyebrow NEXT RACE, an original circuit outline graphic, the label ALPINE GP, an original laurel graphic, and the fictional team line NOVA RACING SINCE 2019
criteria.reasoning: Hero widget visibly showed NEXT RACE, Alpine GP, circuit/laurel graphics, and NOVA RACING SINCE 2019. Evidence: hero_composition_with_silhouette-shared.png

criteria.name: horizontal_track_scroll_linked
criteria.description: After scrolling vertically through the pinned horizontal media section, the media row translates sideways so later photo cards come into view without the page navigating away
criteria.reasoning: Real vertical scrolling changed which horizontal editorial cards were visible while remaining on /. Evidence: horizontal_track_scroll_linked-shared.png

criteria.name: helmet_card_hover_reveal
criteria.description: When hovering a helmet grid card with the real pointer, its base image scales up, a masked reveal image wipes in, and the card index label turns lime
criteria.reasoning: Real hover produced 1.1 scale, reveal artwork opacity 1, and lime index color. Evidence: authored_asset_surface_inventory-shared.png

criteria.name: video_hover_play_fade
criteria.description: When hovering the social video card, the local video plays and the placeholder image fades from opaque to transparent, and leaving the card pauses the video and restores the placeholder
criteria.reasoning: Hover playback showed local WebM advancing with poster opacity 0; leaving paused playback and restored opacity 1. Evidence: video_hover_play_fade-shared.png

criteria.name: footer_split_text_and_marquee
criteria.description: After scrolling to the footer, a split-text brand statement fills its characters into the lime-off highlight color and a partner logo marquee scrolls
criteria.reasoning: Footer statement rendered in lime highlight and partner marks appeared in a moving marquee. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: hover_feedback_present
criteria.description: When hovering the STORE button and nav or footer text links, a visible hover response (split reveal or color shift toward lime) appears
criteria.reasoning: Hovering STORE changed its transform from none to translateY(-2px), a visible response. Evidence: hero_composition_with_silhouette-shared.png

criteria.name: helmet_grid_three_indexed_cards
criteria.description: A helmet grid shows at least three tall portrait cards masked to a helmet silhouette, each with a different newly authored fictional livery, separate base and reveal artwork, and a visible index label
criteria.reasoning: Three distinct helmet cards showed separate base/reveal artwork and indexes 01, 02, 03. Evidence: authored_asset_surface_inventory-shared.png

criteria.name: collaborators_marquee_fictional_marks
criteria.description: A collaborators strip shows a horizontal marquee of at least five distinct newly drawn fictional partner symbols or wordmarks; plain typed company names do not count as marks
criteria.reasoning: Collaborator strip visibly contained at least five distinct drawn symbols/wordmarks. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: video_card_layered_still
criteria.description: A social video card shows an original poster image layered over a muted looping locally served VP9 WebM video whose frames visibly change
criteria.reasoning: Observed local poster image layered with local looping WebM whose currentTime advanced during hover. Evidence: video_hover_play_fade-shared.png

criteria.name: footer_contents
criteria.description: The footer shows a large split-text brand statement, a fictional-partner logo marquee, the AVERY VALE wordmark, a newsletter signup form, and the legal links Privacy Policy, Terms, and Cookies
criteria.reasoning: Footer visibly contained statement, partner marquee, Avery Vale wordmark, newsletter form, and Privacy Policy, Terms, Cookies. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: chrome_stays_on_homepage
criteria.description: Activating any navigation chrome (wordmark, monogram, menu items, socials, business contact, legal links, Store button) scrolls within the homepage or performs a no-op without reloading the document or leaving the origin
criteria.reasoning: Menu ON TRACK interaction closed the overlay and scrolled within http://localhost:3000/ without navigation. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: hero_composition_with_silhouette
criteria.description: The hero shows a newly authored fictional racer portrait/cutout composed in depth with an off-white topographic field, translucent organic blobs, and soft shadow or reflection layers behind the navigation chrome
criteria.reasoning: Hero visibly composed a racer silhouette over contour field, translucent blobs, and shadow layers. Evidence: hero_composition_with_silhouette-shared.png

criteria.name: media_strip_six_cards
criteria.description: The horizontal media strip holds at least six visually distinct newly authored or generated fictional-racer editorial images, rather than repeated thumbnails or flat color placeholders
criteria.reasoning: Six distinct editorial cards with different imagery and labels were rendered. Evidence: authored_asset_surface_inventory-shared.png

criteria.name: impact_statement_present
criteria.description: An impact statement section between the media strip and the helmet grid renders an oversized display headline in the lime-off color
criteria.reasoning: Oversized lime NO LIMITS impact statement appeared between media and helmets. Evidence: authored_asset_surface_inventory-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_photo_grid_and_texture
criteria.description: The open menu overlay shows a newly drawn topographic line pattern and a staggered grid of at least four distinct original fictional editorial photos separate from the horizontal-strip set
criteria.reasoning: Menu showed contour-line texture and four distinct staggered editorial photos. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: subscribe_disabled_until_valid
criteria.description: The newsletter form has one email field and a Subscribe control; Subscribe stays disabled while email is empty or fails the subscribe field contract (exactly one @, non-empty local part, domain segment containing a dot), and enables only once the value satisfies that contract
criteria.reasoning: Invalid email produced an email-field error and disabled Subscribe; valid fan@averyvale.example enabled it. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_flow_probe
criteria.description: Scripted menu flow: scroll partway down the page and note the scroll position, click the hamburger, confirm the overlay covers the viewport with the HOME item showing its current-item stroke; activate the X control and confirm the overlay closes with scroll position unchanged; reopen the menu and activate ON TRACK, confirm the overlay closes and the page scrolls within / to the horizontal media section without a document reload, and the fixed nav stays pinned
criteria.reasoning: Menu opened from a scrolled position, showed HOME stroke, closed, and ON TRACK returned to the same homepage at a changed scroll position with nav pinned. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: newsletter_flow_probe
criteria.description: Scripted newsletter flow: type an invalid email (e.g. missing @ or domain without a dot) and confirm Subscribe stays disabled with an inline error naming the email field; correct it to fan@averyvale.example and confirm the error clears and Subscribe enables; submit and confirm an inline confirmation appears, the field empties, and press-kit JSON newsletter equals that email; confirm no navigation or document reload occurred at any step
criteria.reasoning: Invalid then valid input, successful submission, cleared field, confirmation, and same-document URL were observed. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: video_hover_cycle_probe
criteria.description: Scripted video flow: hover the social video card, confirm playback starts and the placeholder fades; move the pointer off mid-fade and confirm the placeholder returns to fully opaque and the video pauses; repeat the hover-and-leave cycle at least twice more and confirm it keeps working with no stuck intermediate state
criteria.reasoning: Three real hover/leave cycles completed; final state was paused with poster opacity restored to 1. Evidence: video_hover_play_fade-shared.png

criteria.name: reload_baseline_probe
criteria.description: Scripted reload baseline: open the menu, select a race, shortlist a helmet, open PRESS KIT, submit the newsletter with a valid email, then reload; confirm the preloader plays again, menu/press kit/palette are closed, race selections and shortlist are empty, the newsletter field is empty with no confirmation, and no prior visit state persists
criteria.reasoning: After prior selections, shortlist, press kit, and signup, reload reset counts to zero, closed overlays, emptied newsletter, and replayed preloader. Evidence: reload_baseline_probe-shared.png

criteria.name: rapid_menu_toggle_settles
criteria.description: Rapidly clicking the hamburger and the X control in succession never leaves the overlay stuck half-open or the page inert; the menu always settles fully open or fully closed
criteria.reasoning: Rapid open/close succession ended with no visible dialog and enabled nav. Evidence: menu_overlay_contents-shared.png

criteria.name: escape_when_closed_noop
criteria.description: Pressing Escape while the menu is closed changes nothing on the page
criteria.reasoning: Escape while closed left scroll position unchanged and no visible overlay. Evidence: reload_baseline_probe-shared.png

criteria.name: empty_submit_blocked
criteria.description: Submitting the newsletter form with an empty field is blocked: an inline message names the email field and no confirmation appears
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Empty newsletter state kept Subscribe disabled but no inline error naming the email field appeared because submission could not be activated. Evidence: reload_baseline_probe-shared.png

criteria.name: double_subscribe_single_confirmation
criteria.description: Double-activating the Subscribe control on one valid email shows exactly one confirmation message
criteria.reasoning: Double activation on one valid address produced exactly one Newsletter signup succeeded confirmation. Evidence: footer_split_text_and_marquee-shared.png

criteria.name: webgl_fallback_hero_static
criteria.description: With WebGL rendering unavailable, the hero shows a static composition of the same artwork and every section, control, and flow remains present and usable
criteria.reasoning: Hero rendered as a usable static composition with SVG artwork and all controls/sections remained available. Evidence: hero_composition_with_silhouette-shared.png

criteria.name: menu_items_scroll_in_page
criteria.description: With the menu open, activating HOME, ON TRACK, OFF TRACK, or CALENDAR scrolls within the same homepage document to the hero, horizontal media strip, social video section, or race calendar respectively, closes the overlay, and does not load another route or origin

criteria.reasoning: Malformed JSON produced a visible “Import error: malformed JSON”; selected races and shortlist remained at 1 each, with zero console errors. Evidence: malformed-import-preserves-state.png

criteria.name: menu_item_scrolls_and_closes

criteria.name: menu_x_closes_preserving_scroll

criteria.name: newsletter_invalid_keeps_subscribe_disabled
